mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains near zero (below 0.25%) while inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates month-over-month, real rates turn negative. Negative real rates reduce equity discount rates and increase nominal asset valuations, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-06-15, FEDFUNDS=0.08% and CPIAUCSL=270.981, indicating accommodative policy amid rising inflation
regime_hint: post-pandemic monetary accommodation with emerging inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC between the interaction term and forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 6% signals tightening labor market conditions and rising wage pressures. This triggers expectations of future Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 6.0% AND UNRATE has declined > 0.5pp over prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-06-15, UNRATE=5.9%, near pre-pandemic lows
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with Fed tightening expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between UNRATE changes and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while housing starts (HOUST) remain flat or decline, signaling weakening consumer demand and housing investment. This demand contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS year-over-year growth < 2% AND HOUST month-over-month change < 0%
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-05-15, RSAFS=620214M and HOUST=1572K, showing sustained demand
regime_hint: demand deceleration phase following post-shock recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the joint predictive power (measured by Rank IC of the interaction term) falls below 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 1% month-over-month, indicating robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. This supply strength supports corporate profit margins and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-06-15, INDPRO=100.0951, near pre-pandemic levels
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO momentum and 5-day forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window, or the direction of the relationship reverses (becomes negative) in more than 40% of rolling 6-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains above 5.5%, signaling labor market slack and reduced Fed tightening urgency. This combination produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as tightening expectations ease.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-06-15, PAYEMS=145759K and UNRATE=5.9%, showing mixed labor signals
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with moderating labor market momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC with forward returns falls below 0.015 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarter-over-quarter decelerates below 1% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown. This demand deceleration reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-01-15, GDPC1=19086.375B, reflecting strong post-pandemic recovery
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 0.5% month-over-month while the federal funds rate remains unchanged below 0.5%, real rates compress further into negative territory. This monetary squeeze on real returns drives equity investors to demand higher nominal returns, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS < 0.5% AND FEDFUNDS unchanged for prior 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-06-15, CPIAUCSL=270.981 and FEDFUNDS=0.08%, showing policy lag behind inflation
regime_hint: monetary policy lag during inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC falls below 0.015 over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decelerates to negative month-over-month growth while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously contracts, signaling a supply-side shock combined with labor market deterioration. This dual contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < 0% AND PAYEMS month-over-month change < 0K
evidence_refs: fact:As of 2021-06-15, both INDPRO and PAYEMS remain positive, indicating no current supply shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term's Rank IC with forward returns falls below 0.02 over any 12-month rolling window.
